channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Russia's Putin begins a visit to Turkmenistan for forum with regional leaders, including Iran
item.description: ASHGABAT, Turkmenistan (AP) — President Vladimir Putin began a visit to Turkmenistan Friday, speaking at an international forum with Central Asian leaders and the president of Iran.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Jewish organization blasts colleges in billboard ad near San Diego State University to combat antisemitism
item.description: A Jewish organization is blasting a bold message on a billboard near San Diego State University and calling on colleges to fight antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Residents clean up and figure out what's next after Milton
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Florida residents were continuing to repair the damage from Hurricane Milton and figure out what to do next Friday after the storm smashed through coastal communities and tore homes to pieces, flooded streets and spawned a barrage of deadly tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: JULIO CORTEZ, KATE PAYNE and HAVEN DALEY, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: What to know about Hurricane Milton after it slammed Florida's Gulf Coast
item.description: As Hurricane Milton barreled into and across Florida, it spawned dozens of tornadoes, knocked out power to millions, damaged homes, flooded neighborhoods and led to hundreds of rescues. But the damage wasn't as bad as had been feared. Gov.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Massive hole swallows 2 homes in Florida
item.description: The wild scene raised concerns about whether the ground collapse was caused by a sinkhole or water damage from sewage issues.
item.dc:creator: Austin Kellerman, Fox 5

item.title: The Latest: Milton causes widespread power outages and several deaths in Florida
item.description: Hurricane Milton brought powerful winds, a dangerous storm surge and flooding to much of Florida after making landfall along the Gulf Coast as a Category 3 storm. It weakened as it plowed through Florida late Wednesday into Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A former DEA agent is convicted of protecting drug traffickers
item.description: BUFFALO, N.Y. (AP) — A former U.S. Drug Enforcement Administration agent in Buffalo, New York, was convicted of corruption Thursday following a second trial on charges he used his position to protect drug traffickers he believed were associated with organized crime.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden suggests Trump 'get a life' after false hurricane claims
item.description: “Mr. President Trump, former President Trump, get a life man, help these people,” Biden said in remarks on Hurricane Milton from the White House.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: Condemned inmate Richard Moore wants someone other than South Carolina's governor to decide clemency
item.description: A South Carolina inmate scheduled to be executed in just over three weeks is asking a federal judge to take away the power of granting clemency from the governor and place it with a parole board.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Collins, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 19 people in Missouri, Kansas indicted for meth and fentanyl trafficking ring
item.description: Prosecutors said law enforcement seized approximately 100 pounds of meth and two pounds of fentanyl pills from a drug car.
item.dc:creator: Kevin S. Held, NewsNation

item.title: Chicago man charged with assaulting two officers during protests of Netanyahu address to Congress
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An Illinois man was arrested Thursday on charges that he attacked two police officers during protests in Washington, D.C., against Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s address to Congress in July.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indiana growers seeing above average pumpkin crops this fall
item.description: "These are some of the most beautiful pumpkins we've raised in years," said Kyle Spencer, co-owner of Spencer Farm in Noblesville.
item.dc:creator: Scarlett O’Hara, NewsNation

item.title: Disney World and other Orlando parks to reopen Friday after Hurricane Milton shutdown
item.description: Florida theme parks including Disney World, Universal Orlando and SeaWorld say they’ll open their gates on Friday after shutting down for Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Mike Schneider, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Fisher-Price recalls over 2 million infant swings after 5 deaths reported
item.description: A recall has been issued for more than 2 million Fisher-Price infant swings after five deaths were reported.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: More than 2 million without power as Hurricane Milton slams Florida, causes deaths and flooding
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — Hurricane Milton plowed into Florida as a Category 3 storm Wednesday, bringing misery to a coast still ravaged by Helene, pounding cities with winds of over 100 mph (160 kph) after producing a barrage of tornadoes, but sparing Tampa a direct hit.
item.dc:creator: TERRY SPENCER and KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Polluted waste from Florida's fertilizer industry is in the path of Milton's fury
item.description: As Hurricane Milton pummeled Florida’s west coast with powerful winds and flooding rain, environmentalists worry it could scatter the polluted leftovers of the state’s phosphate fertilizer mining industry and other hazardous waste across the peninsula and into vulnerable waterways.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BIESECKER and JASON DEAREN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Taylor Swift donates $5 million to Hurricane Helene and Milton relief efforts
item.description: Taylor Swift donated $5 million to Feeding America’s disaster relief efforts for those affected by ­­Hurricane Helene and Milton.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Why Hurricane Milton became so intense so fast — and why more storms may do the same
item.description: One professor said that "we are seeing noticeable increases in how quickly storms strengthen compared to a couple decades ago.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, KRON4

item.title: Head of international shipping regulator says industry must do more to cut carbon pollution
item.description: HAMBURG, Germany (AP) — For years, the international shipping industry has been criticized for making little progress in reducing the carbon-belching pollution released from the fuels that vessels use in moving most of the cargo that people use every day, such as food, cars and clothing.
item.dc:creator: PETER PRENGAMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2025 Met Gala theme revealed; LeBron James among co-chairs
item.description: The Metropolitan Museum Art has announced the theme for the 2025 Met Gala, formally called the Costume Institute Benefit. The annual spring event's theme will be “Superfine: Tailoring Black Style," according to Vogue. It was inspired by Monica L.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: Review: Samsung latest watch and smart ring
item.description: When it comes to health and fitness tracking, Samsung has you covered. The company has everything from the ultra-affordable to the ultra-rugged - plus a ring that can track your stats 24/7. Their lineup starts with the $200 Galaxy Watch FE, which gives you the basics at an affordable price.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Over 9 million pounds of ready-to-eat meat, poultry recalled: USDA
item.description: The products may be contaminated with Listeria monocytogenes, according to the U.S. Department of Agriculture.
item.dc:creator: Michael Reiner, ABC 4

item.title: Michigan US Senate debate: Rogers, Slotkin race could determine control
item.description: Rogers and Slotkin meet on Tuesday for an hour-long debate hosted by Nexstar's WOOD-TV, beginning at 7 p.m. ET.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Van Gilder, Fox 5

item.title: Hurricane Milton slams Florida as a Category 3 storm; leaving millions in the dark
item.description: Hurricane Milton made landfall Wednesday along Florida’s Gulf Coast as a Category 3 storm, bringing powerful winds, deadly storm surge and potential flooding to much of the state. Milton drew fuel from exceedingly warm Gulf of Mexico waters, twice reaching Category 5 status.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ethel Kennedy, widow of Robert F. Kennedy, hospitalized after suffering stroke, family says
item.description: Ethel Kennedy, 96, is former President John F. Kennedy's sister-in-law and mother to 11 children, including former independent presidential candidate Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: iPhone update allows users to text for help when there's no cell service. How it works
item.description: With Hurricane Milton barreling toward Florida's Gulf Coast, many Tampa Bay area residents are fearing widespread and potentially weeks-long power outages, which could affect cellphone service, as many saw during Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, ABC 4

item.title: Mama bear defeats rival that killed her cub to win Fat Bear Week 2024
item.description: The brown bears truly ate this year, and we mean they ate a lot of salmon at Katmai National Park and Preserve.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Parsons, ABC 4

item.title: Mama bear defeats rival that killed her cub to win Fat Bear Week 2024
item.description: The brown bears truly ate this year, and we mean they ate a lot of salmon at Katmai National Park and Preserve.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Parsons, Fox 5

item.title: VIDEO: Hurricane hunters take turbulent trip through Milton
item.description: Researchers on a hurricane hunter aircraft had a bumpy ride as they flew into Hurricane Milton on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, Fox 5

item.title: TikTok is designed to be addictive to kids and causes them harm, US states' lawsuits say
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia filed lawsuits against TikTok on Tuesday, saying that the popular short-form video app is designed to be addictive to kids and harms their mental health.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Why do people fill their bathtubs with water before a hurricane?
item.description: If you live in a hurricane-prone area, it may be common practice for you. If you live well outside that region, you may be confused by the move.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Pioneers in artificial intelligence win the Nobel Prize in physics
item.description: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Two pioneers of artificial intelligence — John Hopfield and Geoffrey Hinton — won the Nobel Prize in physics Tuesday for helping create the building blocks of machine learning that is revolutionizing the way we work and live but also creates new threats for humanity.
item.dc:creator: DANIEL NIEMANN, SETH BORENSTEIN and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Is it safe to be outside in Hurricane Milton's eye? Here's what experts say
item.description: The eye of a hurricane is usually calm compared to the storm swirling around it — but is it safe to be outside as the eye passes over you?
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, ABC 4

item.title: As Milton takes aim at Florida, why is Tampa Bay so vulnerable to hurricanes?
item.description: The last time the eye of a major storm like Hurricane Milton struck Tampa Bay, in 1921, the city was a sleepy backwater of a few hundred thousand people.
item.dc:creator: TERRY SPENCER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What polling shows about Black voters' views of Harris and Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Black registered voters have an overwhelmingly positive view of Vice President Kamala Harris, but they’re less sure that she would change the country for the better, according to a recent poll from the AP-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Costco gold bars are selling out as prices for the precious metal break records: survey
item.description: Move over, gas and hot dogs. Costco shoppers are snatching up the store's 1-ounce gold bars despite the market's lofty price for the precious metal.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Man has intense stare down with moose before it charges at him
item.description: A man who came face-to-face with a bull moose in the woodlands of Maine narrowly avoided a major mishap after the animal charged after him.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC
